summary: Persistent bilateral knee pain — physiotherapy and NSAIDs failing
fullText: Patient presents with an 8-month history of bilateral knee pain, worse on the left. Reports morning stiffness lasting approximately 45 minutes. Conservative management with 6 weeks of physiotherapy and naproxen 500 mg BID has not provided adequate relief. Functional capacity significantly reduced — difficulty climbing stairs and walking more than two city blocks. No recent fever or joint swelling. BMI 28. Referred for orthopaedic assessment.

summary: MRI left knee: medial compartment osteoarthritis, moderate joint space narrowing
fullText: MRI Left Knee — Findings: Medial compartment osteoarthritis with moderate joint space narrowing. Medial meniscal degeneration without discrete tear. Small effusion. Articular cartilage loss grade III medial tibial plateau. ACL intact. No loose bodies identified.

summary: X-ray both knees: bilateral medial compartment narrowing
fullText: X-ray Bilateral Knees (standing AP): Bilateral medial compartment joint space narrowing, moderate on the left, mild on the right. Marginal osteophytes medial and lateral femoral condyle bilaterally. No acute fracture.

summary: Medications: Naproxen 500 mg BID, Acetaminophen 1 g TID PRN
fullText: Current medications reviewed: Naproxen 500 mg BID (for knee pain, ongoing), Acetaminophen 1000 mg TID PRN. No current anticoagulants. Patient tolerating medications without GI symptoms.
Allergy documented: Penicillin — maculopapular rash. Reaction severity: moderate. Last exposure 2010.

summary: Exertional chest tightness and dyspnoea — ECG changes, cardiology referral indicated
fullText: Patient reports 3-month history of exertional chest tightness and dyspnoea with moderate activity (climbing two flights of stairs, brisk walking). Denies rest pain, palpitations, syncope. Hypertension: controlled on ramipril and amlodipine. Former smoker — 15 pack-years, quit 2019. Family history: father MI at 58. ECG performed in office: non-specific ST-segment changes in leads II, III, aVF. BP 134/84 mmHg. Heart rate 76 bpm, regular. Cardiology referral indicated for further assessment.

summary: ECG: non-specific ST changes in leads II, III, aVF
fullText: 12-lead ECG: Sinus rhythm, heart rate 76 bpm. Non-specific ST-segment changes in leads II, III, aVF. No acute ST elevation. QTc 432 ms. No bundle branch block. Compared to prior ECG (2025-09-10): new ST changes.
Fasting Lipid Panel: Total cholesterol 5.9 mmol/L, LDL 3.8 mmol/L (elevated), HDL 1.1 mmol/L, Triglycerides 2.2 mmol/L. eGFR 74 mL/min/1.73m². HbA1c 5.8%.

summary: Medications: Ramipril 5 mg daily, Amlodipine 5 mg daily, ASA 81 mg daily
fullText: Medications reviewed: Ramipril 5 mg daily (hypertension), Amlodipine 5 mg daily (hypertension), Aspirin 81 mg daily (cardiovascular prophylaxis). No anticoagulants. No known drug allergies.

summary: Fatigue, 5 kg weight loss over 4 months, intermittent RUQ discomfort — workup required
fullText: Patient presents with 4-month history of progressive fatigue, 5 kg unintentional weight loss, and intermittent right upper quadrant discomfort described as dull and non-radiating. No jaundice, haematemesis, melaena, or change in bowel habit. Appetite reduced. No recent travel. No fever. Diabetes managed on metformin. Physical exam: mild RUQ tenderness on deep palpation, no guarding or rigidity. No palpable hepatomegaly. Further workup required. Referral for internal medicine assessment.
Current medications: Metformin 500 mg BID (type 2 diabetes, well-controlled). Allergy: Sulfa drugs — urticaria. No recent bloodwork on file.
Allergy: Sulfonamides (sulfa drugs) — urticaria, moderate reaction. Documented 2018.
